title: identity.rs: secret_bytes() and hpke_secret_bytes() return key material as plain arrays with no Zeroize wrapper
description: secret_bytes() returns [u8; 32] and hpke_secret_bytes() returns [u8; 32] by value. The returned arrays sit on the stack of the caller and are not zeroed on drop. Callers (tui/main.rs extracts hpke_secret with let hpke_secret = identity.hpke_secret_bytes();) hold raw key material in a plain stack array for the lifetime of that binding. The existing issue nie-kef6.103 tracks to_secret_bytes_64 and callers, but the root cause is that these methods should return Zeroizing<[u8;32]> so the zeroize responsibility is not on every caller.
